make a procedural building start from the rectangle on the bottom and divide lines in segments and then extrude and use segments to create different facade effects then divide the total height by the height floor and subdivide to get windows make extrusion use edges to sweep and add profiles analyze text first reinterpret and provide also feedback and analyze the intput and where the potential issues arries and what kind of general itnelligence and system prompt we need to make sure this works better

ANALYSIS OF REQUEST: Your description outlines a facade-driven procedural building workflow. Key ambiguities: (1) 'divide lines in segments' - base perimeter edges vs vertical mullions? (2) 'extrude segments' - edges need thickness before extrusion; better to use SubdivideSurface on faces. (3) 'divide height by floor height' - needs explicit floorHeight slider with formula totalHeight/floorHeight. (4) 'sweep edges with profiles' - requires profile curve + rail edge extraction (EdgesAsCurves → SweepAlongCurve). SYSTEM INTELLIGENCE NEEDED: CAD-semantic NLP that maps 'facade panels'→SubdivideSurface, 'windows'→Boolean difference or inset extrusion, 'floor divisions'→LinearPattern or height formulas. Must infer parametric relationships (floorCount = totalHeight/floorHeight) and choose construction strategy (curve-driven loft vs solid-driven Boolean). Now building a parametric building demonstrating these concepts with anchored geometry and driver sliders.
